summary: Question 1 from arXiv:2106.04894 asks, for arbitrary nonzero vectors $a_i$, which geometric constraints on $S \subset \mathbb{R}^d$ yield non-trivial uniform upper bounds on $\Pr(X \in S)$. The source paper itself answers this for o-minimal sets (those definable in an o-minimal structure) not containing a line segment, giving a bound of $n^{-1/2+o(1)}$. A 2025 paper by Grebennikov and Kwan (arXiv:2505.24699) extends this programme substantially, proving that sets in convex position and algebraic varieties of prescribed dimension and degree also yield such bounds, thereby resolving Conjecture 10.1 of Fox-Kwan-Spink as well as a conjecture of Kwan-Sauermann; the general question of characterising all admissible geometric constraints remains an open research direction.

since_posted.title: Geometric Littlewood–Offord problems via lattice point counting
since_posted.claim: Resolves Conjecture 10.1 of Fox-Kwan-Spink (convex position) and the Kwan-Sauermann conjecture on algebraic varieties, providing new classes of geometric constraints on S that imply anti-concentration bounds of the form asked for in Question 1.

search_queries: Fox Kwan Spink Littlewood-Offord geometric o-minimal 2106.04894
Littlewood-Offord geometric anti-concentration set R^d nonzero vectors 2022 2023 2024
notes: Question 1 is an open-ended research programme rather than a single falsifiable conjecture; new classes of admissible geometric constraints continue to be identified. The 2025 Grebennikov-Kwan paper (2505.24699) is the main follow-up and explicitly cites Fox-Kwan-Spink [16] as the source of the resolved conjecture (their Conjecture 10.1). The general characterisation question remains open.
